methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 24 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Galadyne is a pre-seed stage company in Defense & Security founded by Chandler Luzsicza. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 10 private and public peers, including Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries, alongside public comparables DRSHF and IDEAFORGE.NS.

The analysis applies a single applicable methodology: stage-progression valuation. Using Galadyne's $4.8M total raised as a proxy base value, a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for Pre-Seed to next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $12M. Peer-median analysis was not applicable due to insufficient comparable data—while 24 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, filtering for matching stage and valuation tier yielded fewer than three datapoints, making the approach unreliable.

The triangulated valuation range is $10M to $14M, with a median of $12M. This single-method foundation introduces material uncertainty: the analysis lacks independent triangulation from comparable company multiples or revenue-based approaches. The Pre-Seed stage and frontier nature of the sector limit available benchmarks. Key variables affecting valuation include technology differentiation within the Defense & Security segment, customer traction post-funding, and management team execution capability on the path to Series A.
